task_description:
# RedwoodSDK: Ping / Pong JSX Route ## Background RedwoodSDK (rwsdk) is a server-first React framework for the Cloudflare platform. It runs as a Vite plugin and lets you handle requests with `defineApp`, `render`, and `route` from the `rwsdk/worker` and `rwsdk/router` packages, where route handlers can return either a standard `Response` or JSX that is rendered to HTML on the server. A baseline RedwoodSDK project has already been scaffolded at `/home/user/myproject`. Your job is to add a new HTTP route to the existing app that returns JSX and is rendered as a real HTML page. ## Requirements - Add a new GET route at the path `/ping` to the existing RedwoodSDK app in `src/worker.tsx`. - The `/ping` route must return a JSX element (not a raw `new Response(...)`), so that rwsdk renders it as a full HTML document via the `Document` shell. - The returned JSX must contain the literal text `Pong` wrapped inside an HTML element (for example `

Pong

`). - The existing routes (such as `/`) and the `Document` shell must continue to work; do not break the existing app. - The project must still run under the standard dev server command (`npm run dev`) on the default Vite port `5173`. ## Implementation Hints - Look at `src/worker.tsx` and observe how the existing app uses `defineApp`, `render`, and `route` from `rwsdk/worker` / `rwsdk/router`. - Add a new `route("/ping", ...)` entry inside the same `render(Document, [...])` block so the new route also goes through the shared HTML document shell. - Route handlers can return JSX directly; RedwoodSDK will render it to HTML on the server. No client-side hydration is required for this task. - You do not need to add any new dependencies, change `wrangler.jsonc`, or modify the `Document` component. ## Acceptance Criteria - Project path: `/home/user/myproject` - Start command: `npm run dev` - Port: `5173` - API Endpoints / Routes: - `GET /ping`: - Returns HTTP status `200`. - Response `Content-Type` indicates HTML (for example `text/html`). - Response body is an HTML document whose body contains the literal text `Pong` wrapped in an HTML element (such as `

Pong

`, `

Pong

`, `

Pong

`, etc.). Plain text responses or bodies that contain only the bare string `Pong` without an enclosing HTML element are NOT acceptable. - `GET /` continues to respond with HTTP status `200` (the original route behavior must be preserved).
task_integrations:
- RedwoodSDK (`rwsdk`) — server-first React framework, used via the `rwsdk/worker` and `rwsdk/router` modules.
- Cloudflare Workers / Vite dev server — RedwoodSDK runs as a Vite plugin targeting the Cloudflare Workers runtime; the dev server is started locally with `npm run dev` and no external Cloudflare account is required for this task.
truth:
# Verification Plan

## Setup
1. `cd /home/user/myproject`
2. Make sure no leftover dev server is running on port `5173`. If one is, stop it before starting a new one.
3. Start the dev server with `npm run dev` and wait until it is listening on `http://localhost:5173`.

## Verification Steps
1. **`/ping` returns a JSX-rendered HTML page containing `Pong`**:
   - Request: `GET http://localhost:5173/ping`
   - Expected:
     - HTTP status `200`.
     - `Content-Type` response header contains `text/html`.
     - Response body is a full HTML document (contains an `<html>` tag and a `<body>` tag).
     - Response body matches a pattern like `<(h1|h2|h3|h4|h5|h6|p|div|span|strong|em|b|i)[^>]*>\s*Pong\s*</\1>` (case-insensitive), i.e. the literal text `Pong` appears wrapped inside an HTML element. A body that contains only the bare text `Pong` with no enclosing element fails this check.
2. **The original `/` route is not broken**:
   - Request: `GET http://localhost:5173/`
   - Expected: HTTP status `200`.
3. **Source change is in `src/worker.tsx`**:
   - The file `/home/user/myproject/src/worker.tsx` exists and references both `route` and `/ping` (i.e. the new route is wired in the app definition, not added via some unrelated mechanism).
